skill.name: agents-v2-py
skill.description: Guides Python developers through creating Azure AI Foundry hosted agents that run custom container images with Azure AI Projects SDK.
skill.summary: Build and manage Azure AI Foundry hosted agents with Python container images.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported code execution, network, environment access, weak crypto, and reconnaissance patterns. Manual review found a Markdown documentation skill with benign Python and bash examples, no prompt injection, no credential exfiltration, and no executable payload.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Markdown Backticks and Example Commands
low_findings.description: The external command alerts are Markdown code fences, inline code formatting, and an installation example. The skill does not contain executable scripts or hidden command runners.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are Markdown examples and Python snippets. No mechanism exists in the skill file to execute these commands automatically.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Python Imports Misread as Dynamic Imports
low_findings.description: The dynamic import alerts point to normal Python import blocks for Azure AI Projects model classes. They are static imports used in documentation examples.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations contain import statements only. There is no import expression built from user input or remote content.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Placeholder URLs and Reference Links
low_findings.description: The network alerts are sample Azure endpoints, example MCP server URLs, and public documentation links. No data is transmitted by the skill itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible configuration examples and documentation references. I found no request code or exfiltration endpoint.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Environment Variable Reads for Configuration
low_findings.description: The environment access alerts read AZURE_AI_PROJECT_ENDPOINT for client configuration and pass it into example container settings. The examples also warn against hardcoded secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The environment variable is a project endpoint, not a secret. The skill contains no network call that sends environment values to an unauthorized destination.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Heuristics
low_findings.description: The weak crypto and system reconnaissance alerts do not match the reviewed content. The cited lines contain metadata, parameter tables, and an error table entry.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: No cryptographic algorithms, host enumeration, or system probing code appears at the cited locations. The matches are scanner false positives.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Critical Combination Heuristic
low_findings.description: The critical heuristic combined documentation examples for imports, URLs, and environment variables. Full-file review found no prompt injection, credential theft, obfuscation, or malicious control flow.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The entire file is readable documentation with examples. The risky categories do not combine into an executable attack path.

content.user_title: Build Azure AI Hosted Agents in Python
content.value_statement: Creating container-based Azure AI Foundry agents requires many SDK details. This skill gives focused Python guidance for hosted agents, versions, tools, resources, and async creation.
actual_capabilities: Shows how to install the Azure AI Projects SDK and Azure Identity packages.
Demonstrates client authentication with DefaultAzureCredential and AZURE_AI_PROJECT_ENDPOINT.
Explains creating, listing, and deleting hosted agent versions.
Covers ImageBasedHostedAgentDefinition settings for image, protocol, CPU, memory, tools, and environment variables.
Provides synchronous and asynchronous Python creation patterns.
Maps common hosted agent errors to likely causes and fixes.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not run or deploy agents by itself.
It assumes users already have Azure AI Foundry, ACR, and managed identity access.
It does not validate current Azure service limits or SDK behavior at runtime.
It uses placeholder container images and URLs that must be replaced.

use_cases.title: Create a first hosted agent
use_cases.description: Follow a minimal Python workflow for creating a container-based hosted agent version.

use_cases.title: Configure production agent resources
use_cases.description: Review CPU, memory, protocol, tool, and environment variable settings before deployment.

use_cases.title: Troubleshoot failed hosted agent setup
use_cases.description: Use the error table to identify missing ACR permissions, image issues, or protocol mistakes.

prompt_templates.title: Create a minimal agent
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a minimal Python hosted agent using ImageBasedHostedAgentDefinition and DefaultAzureCredential.

prompt_templates.title: Add tools and environment settings
prompt_templates.prompt: Adapt my Azure AI Foundry hosted agent to include code interpreter, file search, and safe environment variable configuration.

prompt_templates.title: Review deployment settings
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my hosted agent plan for container image tags, CPU, memory, protocol version, and ACR pull permissions.

prompt_templates.title: Design async creation workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an async Python workflow that creates hosted agent versions and handles common Azure AI Foundry setup errors.

output_examples.input: I need to create a hosted agent from my ACR image.
output_examples.output: A concise setup checklist covering SDK installation, endpoint configuration, DefaultAzureCredential, image path, protocol version, and create_version usage.

output_examples.input: My hosted agent fails with ImagePullBackOff.
output_examples.output: A troubleshooting explanation that points to ACR pull permission checks and managed identity role assignment.

output_examples.input: I want to add MCP tools to a hosted agent.
output_examples.output: Guidance for adding MCP tool settings alongside code interpreter and file search without hardcoding secrets.

best_practices: Use specific container image tags for production agent versions.
Store configuration in environment variables or Azure Key Vault, not source files.
Start with small CPU and memory settings, then scale based on measured workload needs.
anti_patterns: Do not use latest image tags for production deployments.
Do not hardcode secrets in hosted agent definitions.
Do not skip ACR pull permission checks before creating an agent version.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps build Azure AI Foundry hosted agents that run custom Python container images.

faq.question: Does it deploy resources automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides implementation guidance and examples, but users run deployment steps themselves.

faq.question: Which SDK package does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers azure-ai-projects with hosted agent support and azure-identity for authentication.

faq.question: Does it include async Python guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes an async pattern using Azure Identity and the async AIProjectClient.

faq.question: How should secrets be handled?
faq.answer: Secrets should not be hardcoded. Use environment variables or Azure Key Vault where appropriate.

faq.question: Why were security scanner alerts dismissed?
faq.answer: The alerts matched documentation examples, imports, URLs, and environment variable reads, not executable malicious behavior.
